items.question: If the ball lands in the opponent's court and then hits the net post, the rally is replayed.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the rule was different.
If the ball lands in and then hits the net post, the returner is at fault for not making the return, so the hitter wins the rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 5.

items.question: Players may ask spectators for help on a call.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the rule said players 'should not' ask spectators.
Now it says 'must not,' which makes the ban mandatory.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 7.

items.question: Spin may be applied to the ball at paddle contact.
items.explanationShort:
In 2026, spin at paddle contact is allowed.
What is still banned is adding spin with the hand or fingers during the release.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 11.

items.question: After missing the return, a player may wait until the next serve to call the ball out.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, that delay was allowed under the wording.
Now the out call still has to be made promptly.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 12.

items.question: The receiving team can win the game on a technical foul.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, only the serving side could get the winning point.
Now the receiving team can win the game on a technical foul.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 11.

items.question: For positioning, a wheelchair's large rear wheels are treated like a standing player's legs.
items.explanationShort:
This wheelchair rule is new for 2026.
For positioning, the large rear wheels are what count.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 18.

items.question: A wheelchair player must keep at least one buttock on the seat when striking the ball.
items.explanationShort:
This wheelchair rule is new for 2026.
If the player lifts fully off the seat when hitting, it is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 17.

items.question: If an extra ball a player is carrying is visible to an opponent during a live rally, it is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the extra ball just had to be hidden.
Now a visible extra ball during a live rally is specifically a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 22.

items.question: A time-out must be called clearly by voice, hand signal, or both.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the rule mainly said when to call time-out.
Now it also says how to call it: by voice, hand signal, or both.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 24.

items.question: If a player calls time-out after the serve has been hit, it is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the rule focused on calling time-out before the next serve.
Now a late time-out call after the serve is explicitly a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 24.

items.question: In rally scoring, the receiving team can win the game by winning the rally at game point.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the receiving team could not win at game point.
Now either team can win the game on the rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 16.

items.question: A ball may be hit more than once if one player makes one continuous, single-direction stroke.
items.explanationShort:
This includes rare triple-hit cases.
The stroke still has to be one continuous stroke in one direction by one player.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 10.

items.question: In doubles, if one player retires after a medical time-out, the partner may keep playing.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, this doubles situation was not stated clearly.
Now the retiring player's partner may continue alone.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 13.

items.question: On a wheelchair serve, the front small wheels may extend into the court at contact.
items.explanationShort:
This wheelchair serving rule is new for 2026.
Only the large rear wheels have to stay behind the baseline.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 18.

items.question: An assistive device must not be used to hit the ball or intentionally alter its flight.
items.explanationShort:
This adaptive standing rule is new for 2026.
For example, a crutch or cane cannot be used to hit a live ball.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 21.

items.question: Dangerous paddle or ball abuse can lead to a match forfeit even if no one is hit.
items.explanationShort:
That's a conduct penalty, not a rally fault.
Putting a person or facility property at risk can still lead to a match forfeit.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 23.

items.question: If an assistive device comes loose during a rally, the rally must continue to completion.
items.explanationShort:
This adaptive standing rule is new for 2026.
A loose brace or device does not stop the rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 21.

items.question: An adaptive standing player must declare the two-bounce allowance before the match starts.
items.explanationShort:
This adaptive standing rule is new for 2026.
It must be declared before the match and then used for the rest of that match.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 21.

items.question: Except for the single-foot propulsion exception, a wheelchair player's feet or legs must not touch the ground during a live ball, even by accident.
items.explanationShort:
This wheelchair fault rule is new for 2026.
Except for the single-foot propulsion rule, even accidental ground contact is still a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 17.

items.question: If a time-out is not clearly called by voice or hand signal, a referee may issue a warning.
items.explanationShort:
This warning rule is new for 2026.
For example, leaving the court without clearly calling time-out can trigger it.
2026 USA Pickleball Rulebook Change Document, page 24.

items.question: If the server's foot is beyond the imaginary extension of the sideline, it is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
The server's feet must stay within the correct serving area.
Stepping outside the serving area boundary is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.A.3.a.

items.question: The rally may still stand if the wrong player returns the serve.
items.explanationShort:
This wrong-receiver rule was clarified in 2025.
For example, the wrong doubles partner cannot take the return.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.C.1.c.

items.question: The serve may be hit straight ahead.
items.explanationShort:
The serve must go diagonally crosscourt.
Serving straight ahead is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.E.

items.question: For a volley serve, the paddle may move in any direction at contact.
items.explanationShort:
Before 2026, the rule focused on the arm moving in an upward arc.
Now the paddle itself must move in an upward arc at contact.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.C.1.

items.question: For a volley serve, the paddle head must not be above the server's wrist joint at contact.
items.explanationShort:
The paddle head must be at or below the wrist.
Above the wrist joint at contact is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.C.2.

items.question: For a volley serve, the ball must be below the server's waist at contact.
items.explanationShort:
The ball can't be at or above waist level when struck.
Above the waist is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.C.3.

items.question: A volley serve must be made with a forehand motion.
items.explanationShort:
Both forehand and backhand motions are legal.
The drop serve also allows both motions.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.C.4.

items.question: For a drop serve, the server may toss the ball upward before letting it bounce.
items.explanationShort:
The ball must be dropped from a natural height only.
Propelling it in any direction before contact is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rules 7.D.1-7.D.2.

items.question: A drop serve becomes illegal if the ball bounces twice before it is hit.
items.explanationShort:
There's no limit on how many times the ball can bounce.
There's also no restriction on where it bounces.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rules 7.D.3-7.D.4.

items.question: A server is allowed to have both feet off the ground at contact.
items.explanationShort:
One foot must be grounded behind the baseline.
This applies to both volley and drop serves.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.A.1.

items.question: A server's foot may touch the baseline when striking the ball.
items.explanationShort:
No foot can touch the court, including the baseline, at contact.
The server has to stay behind it until the ball is struck.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.A.2.

items.question: If the receiver cannot see the ball release, the receiver may call for a replay.
items.explanationShort:
The receiver can call a replay in this situation.
The call must be made before the return of serve.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.B.3.a.

items.question: A served ball that lands on the non-volley zone (NVZ) line is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
The serve has to clear the NVZ (kitchen) entirely, including its lines.
Landing on the NVZ line counts as landing in the NVZ.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.E.2.

items.question: If the serve hits the receiver before landing, the server loses the rally.
items.explanationShort:
The fault goes against the receiver, not the server.
This includes anything worn or carried by the receiver or their partner.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.E.5.

items.question: If the serve hits the server's partner, the server's team loses the rally.
items.explanationShort:
It's a fault if the ball hits the server or their partner.
That includes anything worn or carried by either of them.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.E.4.

items.question: If the served ball hits the net post and lands in the correct service court, the serve is good.
items.explanationShort:
Hitting a permanent object before landing is a fault.
The net is allowed, but the net post is not.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.E.3.

items.question: A server may serve as soon as the previous rally ends.
items.explanationShort:
The server has to wait until the full score is called before serving.
If you serve before the score is called, it's a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.C.

items.question: A server's foot may contact the baseline at the moment the paddle strikes the ball.
items.explanationShort:
Touching the baseline at contact is a fault.
The foot must be fully behind the baseline.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.A.2.a.

items.question: A server has 10 seconds after the score is called to serve.
items.explanationShort:
If you go over the 10-second count, it's a fault.
The clock starts after the score call is completed.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.D.1.

items.question: The drop serve must follow the volley serve's motion rules.
items.explanationShort:
The volley serve has arc, wrist, and waist rules.
The drop serve has none of those restrictions.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rules 7.C, 7.D.

items.question: The receiver must be ready before the server serves.
items.explanationShort:
Players signal 'not ready' before the score call.
After the call, the server has 10 seconds to serve.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.A.

items.question: A server may impart spin on the ball during the release.
items.explanationShort:
Spin during the release isn't allowed.
Spin at paddle contact is allowed.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.B.1.

items.question: Letting the ball roll off the paddle face before the serve is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Gravity-caused rolling off the paddle is allowed.
Only deliberate spin during the release is banned.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.B.1.

items.question: A serve that touches the net is automatically a fault.
items.explanationShort:
The served ball can touch the net before landing.
A net touch does not make the serve a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 7.E.

items.question: If the wrong score is called and the rally is completed, the result of the rally stands.
items.explanationShort:
A wrong score call doesn't erase a rally that's already over.
The score must be corrected before the next serve.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.F.4.

items.question: Stopping a rally to challenge the score is always allowed.
items.explanationShort:
If you stop a rally to challenge a correct score, it's a fault.
Only a wrong-score challenge before the return of serve is safe.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.F.2.

items.question: In doubles, the starting server's position is on the right side when the team's score is zero or even.
items.explanationShort:
Position is based on the team's score, not individual.
This applies after each side out.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.B.3.

items.question: In doubles, both players on a team serve before a side out is declared.
items.explanationShort:
Each server serves until their team loses a rally.
A side out follows the second server's lost rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.B.

items.question: A served ball that lands on the centerline of the service court is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
The centerline is part of the service court and counts as in.
Sideline and baseline of the service court also count as in.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.4.f.

items.question: In doubles, the score is called as two numbers.
items.explanationShort:
In doubles, three numbers are called, not two.
The third number is the server number: one or two.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.2.

items.question: In singles, the receiver's score is called first.
items.explanationShort:
The server's score is always called first.
Reversing the order would be a wrong score call.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.1.

items.question: The receiver normally calls the score.
items.explanationShort:
The server normally calls the score, not the receiver.
That's true in both singles and doubles.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.

items.question: In doubles, the server's partner may call the score at any time.
items.explanationShort:
The partner may call only if the server cannot.
It's not a free choice for the partner.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.2.a.

items.question: Saying 'not ready' counts as a valid ready signal.
items.explanationShort:
Only physical signals count here, like raising the paddle.
Saying 'not ready' is not a valid signal.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.A.1.

items.question: After the score call starts, a 'not ready' signal stops play.
items.explanationShort:
Once the score call starts, a 'not ready' signal no longer stops play.
Only a hinder signal still counts at that point.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.A.2.

items.question: The score may be called before all players are in position.
items.explanationShort:
The score must wait until all players are ready.
Calling early can cause a replay or fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.

items.question: A server may serve while the score is still being called.
items.explanationShort:
The full score has to be called before the serve.
If you serve during the call, it's a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.C.1.

items.question: If the serving team changes sides after the score is called, the score must be called again.
items.explanationShort:
The server has to let all players reposition before re-calling the score.
Re-calling before players can reposition isn't allowed.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.D.2.

items.question: After the score call starts, verbal indicators like 'stop' or 'wait' are recognized.
items.explanationShort:
Words like 'stop' or 'wait' are recognized.
They can halt play to flag a wrong score.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.E.

items.question: A player may stop the rally to challenge the score after the return of serve.
items.explanationShort:
A late score challenge is a fault.
It has to happen before the return of serve.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.F.3.

items.question: In doubles, the first score call of the game is 'zero – zero – one.'
items.explanationShort:
The first doubles score call is 'zero–zero–two.'
The opening team gets only one server before the first side out.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.2.

items.question: Before the serve is hit, a player may ask their opponent for the correct score.
items.explanationShort:
A player may ask the opponent for the correct score.
This is allowed before the serve is hit.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.F.

items.question: If the wrong score is called and the rally is stopped before the return of serve, the rally must be replayed.
items.explanationShort:
The rally is replayed with the correct score.
A late challenge results in a fault instead.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.F.1.

items.question: The 10-second count starts when the server begins calling the score.
items.explanationShort:
The count starts after the call is completed.
It doesn't begin when the call starts.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.D.

items.question: In singles, a game always starts with the score call 'zero – zero.'
items.explanationShort:
Every singles game starts with 'zero–zero.'
Server's score is always called first.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.1.

items.question: In standard scoring, the receiving team can score by winning a rally.
items.explanationShort:
In standard scoring, points are scored only when serving.
A technical foul is the only exception.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 4.A.

items.question: In standard scoring, the receiving team scores a point when the serving team loses a rally.
items.explanationShort:
In standard scoring, the receiving team does not score by winning a rally.
Receivers get a point only through a technical foul on the serving team.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 4.A.

items.question: The court must be a rectangle measuring 20 feet (6.10 m) wide and 44 feet (13.41 m) long.
items.explanationShort:
The same dimensions apply in both singles and doubles.
Measurements are made to the outside edge of the lines.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.1.

items.question: The net is 36 inches high across the full width of the court.
items.explanationShort:
The net is 36 inches at sidelines, 34 at center.
It sags slightly in the middle by design.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rules 3.B.6-3.B.7.

items.question: Some approved pickleball balls are restricted to indoor play only.
items.explanationShort:
All approved balls are acceptable for both indoor and outdoor play.
The choice of ball is a preference, not a rule restriction.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.1.

items.question: A pickleball ball may have multiple colors.
items.explanationShort:
The ball must be one uniform color.
A manufacturer logo or name is the only exception.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.3.

items.question: The non-volley zone (NVZ) extends 7 feet (2.13 m) from the net on each side.
items.explanationShort:
The NVZ (kitchen) is 7 feet from the net.
The NVZ line itself is part of the zone.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.4.c.

items.question: A ball landing on the non-volley zone (NVZ) line is considered in the NVZ.
items.explanationShort:
All lines bounding the NVZ (kitchen) are part of the zone itself.
This applies to all lines on all four sides of the NVZ.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.4.c.

items.question: A pickleball ball may have a textured surface.
items.explanationShort:
The ball must have a smooth surface and be free of texturing.
A slight ridge at the seam is the only exception allowed.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.5.

items.question: A pickleball ball may have a slight ridge at the seam.
items.explanationShort:
A seam ridge is allowed if it doesn't affect flight.
That's the only exception to the smooth rule.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.5.

items.question: A paddle may not exceed 17 inches (43.18 cm) in length.
items.explanationShort:
Length plus width must not exceed 24 inches.
Only length and combined dimensions are regulated.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.D.1.

items.question: There is a maximum weight limit for pickleball paddles.
items.explanationShort:
There's no weight restriction for paddles in the rulebook.
Only length and combined dimensions are regulated.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.D.1.

items.question: A center strap is required on all pickleball nets.
items.explanationShort:
A center strap is optional, not required.
It adjusts the net to 34 inches at center.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.B.5.

items.question: Court lines must be 2 inches (5.08 cm) wide.
items.explanationShort:
All court lines must be 2 inches wide.
That applies to every line on the court.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.4.e.

items.question: Court lines may be any color, even if similar to the playing surface.
items.explanationShort:
Lines must clearly contrast with the surface color.
All lines must also be the same color as each other.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.4.e.

items.question: A pickleball ball must have between 26 and 40 circular holes.
items.explanationShort:
The number of holes affects the ball's flight characteristics.
Balls outside this range are not approved for play.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.5.

items.question: The net must be at least 30 inches (76.2 cm) tall from bottom to top.
items.explanationShort:
The net must be at least 30 inches tall.
This is separate from the playing height above court.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.B.3.

items.question: The net must be made of a specific approved material.
items.explanationShort:
Any mesh fabric is acceptable for the net.
The only requirement is that balls can't pass through.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.B.1.

items.question: A pickleball ball must have a manufacturer or supplier name or logo on its surface.
items.explanationShort:
The marking must be printed or embossed on the ball surface.
This is required for all approved balls.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.4.

items.question: The minimum playing surface area is 30 feet (9.14 m) wide and 60 feet (18.29 m) long.
items.explanationShort:
The minimum playing surface is 30 × 60 feet.
The court itself is only 20 × 44 feet.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.A.3.

items.question: Net posts should be 22 feet (6.71 m) apart, measured from inside edge to inside edge.
items.explanationShort:
A tolerance of ±1.0 inch is allowed.
Each post must not exceed 3 inches in diameter.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.B.2.

items.question: A paddle's hitting surface may have small indentations to improve grip on the ball.
items.explanationShort:
Indentations on the hitting surface are banned.
They could let a player add extra spin.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.D.3.

items.question: A pickleball ball must be between 2.87 inches (7.29 cm) and 2.97 inches (7.54 cm) in diameter.
items.explanationShort:
Balls outside this diameter range aren't approved for play.
The out-of-round variance also can't exceed +/- 0.020 inch (0.51 mm).
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.D.3.

items.question: A pickleball ball must weigh between 0.78 and 0.935 ounces (22.1 and 26.5 grams).
items.explanationShort:
Balls outside this weight range are not approved for play.
Both minimum and maximum weights are strictly enforced.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.D.4.

items.question: A pickleball paddle must be made of rigid, non-compressible material.
items.explanationShort:
The material must be rigid and deemed safe.
Flexible or compressible faces aren't allowed.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.1.

items.question: A pickleball ball must bounce between 30 and 34 inches (76.2 and 86.4 cm) when dropped from 78 inches (198.1 cm).
items.explanationShort:
The bounce test uses a granite surface plate.
Test temperature must be 70°F (±5°F).
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.D.5.

items.question: There is no restriction on paddle handle length.
items.explanationShort:
The handle may be any length within the overall paddle dimensions.
Only the combined length and width cannot exceed 24 inches (60.96 cm).
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.3.

items.question: Adding weight to a paddle makes it non-compliant.
items.explanationShort:
Adding weight is a permitted alteration.
Weighted tape is one allowed method.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.D.

items.question: Decals and tape may not extend more than 1.0 inch (2.54 cm) onto the paddle face from the grip.
items.explanationShort:
Decals and tape can't go any farther onto the face.
The hitting surface must remain unaltered beyond that.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.5.b.

items.question: Decals and tape may extend up to 0.5 inch (1.27 cm) inside the paddle's outer edge.
items.explanationShort:
Beyond 0.5 inch inside the edge is not allowed.
Edge guard presence shifts the limit inward.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.5.b.

items.question: Any writing on the paddle face makes the paddle non-compliant.
items.explanationShort:
Some writing on the face is allowed.
Name and contact information are explicitly permitted.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.5.c.

items.question: A player may have another player's autograph on their paddle's playing surface.
items.explanationShort:
Autographs on the paddle face are allowed.
This is an explicitly permitted identification marking.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.5.c.

items.question: A paddle may include electronic features to assist the player during play.
items.explanationShort:
Electronic communications and power assistance are strictly prohibited.
The paddle must provide no mechanical or electronic advantage during play.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.D.5.

items.question: Changing the grip size of a paddle makes it non-compliant.
items.explanationShort:
Grip size changes are explicitly permitted.
Grip wraps and inserts are allowed.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.5.a.

items.question: Replacing the edge guard on a paddle makes it non-compliant.
items.explanationShort:
Edge guard tape is a permitted player alteration.
Several modifications to commercial paddles are allowed.
2025 USA Pickleball Equipment Standards Manual, Rule 2.E.5.a.

items.question: Any ball design can be used in pickleball as long as it meets size and weight requirements.
items.explanationShort:
The ball design must be specifically approved by USA Pickleball.
Size and weight alone aren't enough for approval.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 3.C.2.

items.question: In doubles, the player closest to the ball makes the line call.
items.explanationShort:
Either or both partners may make a line call.
No rule restricts it to the closer player.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.A.

items.question: If a player is unsure whether a ball is out, they may call it out anyway.
items.explanationShort:
If you can't promptly call it out, it's in.
Questionable calls must always be resolved in favor of the opponent.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.D.

items.question: A player must clearly see a space between the line and the ball to call it out.
items.explanationShort:
No clear space between ball and line means 'in.'
This prevents uncertain out calls.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.E.

items.question: A ball that touches a court line during a rally is out.
items.explanationShort:
Any ball touching a court line is considered in.
Only balls landing fully outside a line are out.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.B.

items.question: An 'out' call must be made promptly by voice, hand signal, or both.
items.explanationShort:
A delayed out call may not be recognized.
The call has to come before the opponent hits back.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.F.

items.question: If doubles partners disagree on a line call, the ball is considered out.
items.explanationShort:
When partners disagree on a line call, the ball is considered in.
This gives the benefit of the doubt to the opponent.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.H.

items.question: A player may overrule their own line call to their own disadvantage.
items.explanationShort:
Players may override calls to their own disadvantage.
Overriding to gain an advantage is not allowed.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.G.

items.question: A player may ask their opponent's opinion on a line call.
items.explanationShort:
A player can defer a line call to the opponent.
The opponent's clear decision then stands.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.I.

items.question: A player may change their mind on a line call after the next serve is hit.
items.explanationShort:
Once the next serve is hit, the call is final.
Any change must happen before that serve.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.I.

items.question: In rally scoring, a point is scored on every rally, regardless of who served.
items.explanationShort:
Every rally awards a point in rally scoring.
This differs from standard side-out scoring.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 14.A.2.

items.question: A player may accept a spectator's line call if the spectator volunteered it.
items.explanationShort:
Spectators must not be consulted on any call.
It doesn't matter whether the spectator volunteered or was asked.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.J.

items.question: When a line call is questionable, the benefit of the doubt goes to the opponent.
items.explanationShort:
Questionable calls must always be resolved in favor of the opponent.
This is a core principle of pickleball's code of ethics.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.D.

items.question: Saying 'out' while the ball is still in the air counts as a line call.
items.explanationShort:
Partner communication in the air is not a call.
A line call is only valid after the ball lands.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.F.4.

items.question: After an 'out' call is made, play must stop immediately.
items.explanationShort:
A line call makes the ball dead immediately.
Play can't continue after a valid out call.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.F.2.

items.question: Players are responsible for making line calls on their opponent's end of the court.
items.explanationShort:
Players call lines on their own end of the court only.
Calling lines on the opponent's end isn't a player's responsibility.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.A.

items.question: If a player asks the opponent's opinion and the opponent declines to make a call, the ball is out.
items.explanationShort:
No definitive call from the opponent means 'in.'
Only a clear 'in' or 'out' overrides the call.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.I.

items.question: If a player returns the ball, they may still call it out after the opponent hits it back.
items.explanationShort:
After the opponent hits back, the out right is lost.
The call must come before the opponent strikes.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.F.3.

items.question: Players must strive for accuracy when making line calls.
items.explanationShort:
Accuracy is a core obligation under pickleball's code of ethics.
Questionable calls must always favor the opponent, not the caller.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.D.

items.question: If no player makes an 'out' call, the ball is considered in.
items.explanationShort:
If you can't call it out, it's in.
Silence on a line call always defaults to in.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 8.D.

items.question: Play may continue after a fault.
items.explanationShort:
A fault immediately stops play and ends the rally.
The rally ends the moment a fault occurs.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.

items.question: A called hinder stops the rally.
items.explanationShort:
Any player may call a hinder to stop play.
Example: a ball rolling in from another court.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.C.

items.question: Players are expected to call faults on themselves or their partner.
items.explanationShort:
Faults must be called when committed or detected.
Waiting until after the rally is too late.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.2.

items.question: A player may call a non-volley zone (NVZ) fault on their opponent.
items.explanationShort:
NVZ and service foot faults may be called on opponents.
These are the only enforceable faults on others.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.3.

items.question: A player may enforce a double-bounce fault on their opponent.
items.explanationShort:
Only NVZ and foot faults are enforceable on opponents.
All other faults are decided by the committing player.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.4.

items.question: A fault call must be made before the next serve is hit.
items.explanationShort:
A fault should be called as soon as detected.
After the next serve, it can no longer be enforced.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.3.a.

items.question: A ball rolling in from another court during a rally is a valid hinder.
items.explanationShort:
An outside object unexpectedly entering the court is a valid hinder.
The rally must be replayed when a valid hinder is called.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.C.

items.question: A player who misses a shot may call a hinder to replay the rally.
items.explanationShort:
A hinder must be caused by unexpected outside interference.
Missing a shot isn't a valid reason to call a hinder.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.C.

items.question: Only the player directly affected may call a hinder.
items.explanationShort:
Any player on either team may call a hinder.
The call is not limited to the affected player.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.C.1.

items.question: A net system malfunction during a rally is a hinder.
items.explanationShort:
Any malfunction of a net system during play is considered a hinder.
The rally must be replayed when a valid hinder is called.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.J.

items.question: A player who is distracted by an opponent's loud noise may call a hinder.
items.explanationShort:
Opponent distraction is a fault, not a hinder.
Hinders come from outside interference only.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.C.

items.question: When two teams disagree on a fault call, the calling team wins the point.
items.explanationShort:
If teammates disagree on a fault, the rally is replayed.
Neither team wins or loses the point.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.3.b.

items.question: When doubles partners disagree on a fault call, the benefit of the doubt goes to their opponents.
items.explanationShort:
Partner disagreement on a fault always favors the opposing team.
This mirrors the same principle that applies to line calls.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.B.3.c.

items.question: A dead ball is no longer in play.
items.explanationShort:
A dead ball means play has stopped.
Neither team can keep the rally going.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Section 2.

items.question: Stopping a rally before it ends is always a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Some reasons to stop a rally are valid exceptions.
A hinder, a position error, or a score challenge can qualify.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.A.1.

items.question: A player may stop a rally to call a hinder without committing a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Calling a hinder is a valid reason to stop play.
A valid hinder results in a replayed rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.A.1.

items.question: A player may stop a rally to identify a position error without committing a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Correctly identifying a position error stops play validly.
An incorrect claim is a fault on the claimant.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.A.1.

items.question: A standard time-out may last up to 2 minutes.
items.explanationShort:
A standard time-out lasts up to 1 minute.
Play may resume early if all players are ready.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 21.A.5.

items.question: The rally may continue after it has been stopped if both teams agree.
items.explanationShort:
Once play has been stopped, the rally can't continue.
Stopping a rally prematurely without a valid reason is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 9.A.1.

items.question: The serve and the return of serve must each bounce before being returned.
items.explanationShort:
This is called the two-bounce rule.
Violating it on either side is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.A.

items.question: A player must return the ball before a second bounce.
items.explanationShort:
Failing to return before the second bounce is a fault.
This applies after the first two shots of a rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.B.

items.question: A player may stop play immediately because they think the ball is broken.
items.explanationShort:
Play must continue even if the ball seems broken.
Players may check and challenge after the rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.G.

items.question: In doubles, one shot may legally contact both partners' paddles.
items.explanationShort:
Only one player on a team may hit each shot.
Both partners hitting the same stroke is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.D.2.

items.question: If a player is injured during a rally, play must continue until the rally ends.
items.explanationShort:
An injury doesn't stop play mid-rally.
The rally must be completed before any medical attention can be given.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.H.

items.question: Players must wait for a time-out to hydrate.
items.explanationShort:
Quick hydration between rallies is permitted.
It must not disrupt the flow of the game.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.K.

items.question: If a player's hat falls onto their end of the court, the rally ends if the ball hits the hat.
items.explanationShort:
Items on a player's side don't stop play.
The rally continues even if the ball hits a fallen item.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.J.

items.question: If a player's shoe comes off during a rally, play must continue until the rally ends.
items.explanationShort:
Equipment and apparel problems don't stop play mid-rally.
The rally must be completed before addressing the problem.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.I.

items.question: A player must not distract their opponent when the opponent is about to hit the ball.
items.explanationShort:
Deliberate distraction is a fault against the player who distracted.
Examples include loud noises, stomping feet, or waving the paddle erratically.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.F.

items.question: Switching the paddle from one hand to the other during a rally is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Switching hands during a rally is allowed.
The player must still be holding the paddle at contact.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 12.B.

items.question: A player may catch the ball on their paddle and redirect it during a rally.
items.explanationShort:
Catching or carrying the ball on the paddle is a fault.
The ball must bounce away from the paddle on contact.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 12.C.

items.question: A player must not use or carry more than one paddle during a rally.
items.explanationShort:
Carrying more than one paddle is a fault.
That applies even if the extra paddle is unused.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 12.A.

items.question: A player may throw their paddle to hit the ball during a rally.
items.explanationShort:
The player must have possession of the paddle when hitting the ball.
Throwing the paddle to make contact is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 12.B.1.

items.question: If a player's shot hits the fence before landing in the court, it is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
A ball hitting a permanent object first is a fault.
It must land in court without contact first.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.C.4.

items.question: If all players agree a broken ball affected the rally, the rally must be replayed.
items.explanationShort:
All players must agree that the break affected the outcome.
If there is no agreement, the result of the rally stands.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.G.1.

items.question: Playing a bounced ball is the only reason a player may enter the non-volley zone (NVZ).
items.explanationShort:
Entering the NVZ (kitchen) is allowed at any time.
The only restriction is volleying while in it.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.

items.question: A player may volley the ball while standing in the non-volley zone (NVZ).
items.explanationShort:
Volleying the ball while in the NVZ (kitchen) is a fault.
The player must be completely outside the NVZ to volley.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.

items.question: If a player's momentum carries them into the non-volley zone (NVZ) after a volley, it is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
Momentum into the NVZ (kitchen) after a volley is a fault.
This applies even after the ball has become dead.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.2.

items.question: After entering the non-volley zone (NVZ), a player may volley the ball as soon as one foot is outside the NVZ.
items.explanationShort:
Both feet must be completely outside the NVZ (kitchen) before volleying.
One foot outside is not sufficient to volley legally.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.3.

items.question: If the ball becomes soft during a rally, the result of the rally is replayed.
items.explanationShort:
A soft or degraded ball doesn't cause a replay.
Only a broken ball agreed on by all can.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 10.G.2.

items.question: If a volleying player's partner touches the non-volley zone (NVZ), it is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
The partner must not touch the NVZ (kitchen) either.
This applies at the moment of the volley.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.1.

items.question: A player may jump from outside the non-volley zone (NVZ), volley the ball in the air, and land inside the NVZ.
items.explanationShort:
Landing in the NVZ (kitchen) after volleying is a fault.
The player must land completely outside.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.2.

items.question: A standard time-out may be called before a match starts.
items.explanationShort:
A standard time-out cannot be called before a match starts.
You can only call one once the match is underway.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 21.A.1.

items.question: In rally scoring doubles, the score is called as three numbers.
items.explanationShort:
Rally scoring uses two numbers, not three.
There's no server number in rally scoring.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 14.A.3.

items.question: A player may touch the non-volley zone (NVZ) with their paddle before hitting a volley.
items.explanationShort:
NVZ (kitchen) contact is only banned during volleying.
Touching it before the volley is not a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.

items.question: Jumping from inside the non-volley zone (NVZ) to volley the ball in the air is legal.
items.explanationShort:
Both feet must be outside the NVZ (kitchen) first.
Jumping from inside to volley is a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 11.A.3.

items.question: A standard pickleball game is played to 15 points.
items.explanationShort:
A standard game is played to 11 points.
15 and 21 are optional tournament formats.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 15.C.1.

items.question: A team wins as soon as it reaches 11 points, even with only a 1-point lead.
items.explanationShort:
A game must be won by at least 2 points.
Reaching 11 is not enough at 11-10.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 15.C.

items.question: A pickleball game may be played to 15 or 21 points in some tournament formats.
items.explanationShort:
One game to 15 or 21 points is an allowed tournament format.
All formats still require a win by 2 points.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 15.C.1.c.

items.question: The standard tournament format is best 2-of-3 games to 11 points.
items.explanationShort:
This is the standard for USA Pickleball sanctioned events.
Best 3-of-5 or one game to 15/21 are also allowed.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 15.C.1.a.

items.question: In a round robin format, players are eliminated after a loss.
items.explanationShort:
Every participant faces every other in round robin.
Elimination formats are single or double elimination.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 15.B.1.

items.question: In a double elimination format, after losing one match, a player plays in a consolation bracket.
items.explanationShort:
A first loss moves the player to consolation bracket.
Elimination requires a second loss.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 15.B.1.

items.question: A ball may be returned around the outside of the net post.
items.explanationShort:
This is called an around-the-post (ATP) shot.
The ball does not need to go over the net.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.C.

items.question: If the ball bounces in your court and returns over the net on its own, you lose the rally.
items.explanationShort:
The player may reach over to hit the returned ball.
The ball must have crossed back first.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.I.2.

items.question: Players should avoid wearing clothing that closely matches the ball color.
items.explanationShort:
Matching the ball color makes it harder to see.
This is a fair play principle.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Section 1.

items.question: In tournament play, shoes must have soles that do not mark or damage the playing surface.
items.explanationShort:
Inappropriate footwear can be required to be changed by the Tournament Director.
Refusing to change apparel can result in a match forfeit.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.B.2.

items.question: A player may be required to change their clothing if it approximates the color of the ball.
items.explanationShort:
A Tournament Director can require an apparel change during a match.
A referee time-out is used for the change.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.B.3.

items.question: In doubles tournament play, the starting server must wear a visible form of identification.
items.explanationShort:
The form of identification is determined by the Tournament Director.
It has to stay visible to officials and players throughout the match.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.C.

items.question: A side out occurs when the serving team wins a rally.
items.explanationShort:
A side out occurs when the serve is lost.
The serve then goes to the opposing team.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Section 2.

items.question: In doubles, the starting server is treated as server 2 for the first service sequence of the game.
items.explanationShort:
Only one player on the opening team serves before the first side out.
After that opening sequence, service goes back to the normal first-server then second-server pattern.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.B.2.

items.question: If the ball hits the net and lands in bounds, play stops and the rally is replayed.
items.explanationShort:
The ball stays in play if it lands in bounds.
Net contact alone does not stop the rally.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.A.

items.question: If the ball hits the cable at the top of the net and lands in bounds, it remains in play.
items.explanationShort:
The cable or rope at the top counts as the net.
The ball stays in play if it lands in bounds.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.A.

items.question: A serve that clips the top of the net and lands in the correct service court is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
A net serve that lands in the correct court is good.
There's no let rule in pickleball.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.A.

items.question: In singles, the server stays on the same side for every serve.
items.explanationShort:
The server alternates sides after each point scored.
The score determines which side to serve from.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.A.2.

items.question: In singles, a side out occurs after the server loses a rally.
items.explanationShort:
The server immediately loses the serve after a lost rally.
Unlike doubles, there is no second server in singles.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.A.3.

items.question: A ball may pass between the net and the net post during play.
items.explanationShort:
The ball has to go over or around the net post.
If it goes through the gap between the net and post, it's a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.D.

items.question: During a match, when a player is discovered playing with a non-compliant paddle, a match forfeit will be imposed.
items.explanationShort:
A non-compliant paddle during play results in an automatic forfeit.
Switching without penalty is only allowed before the match starts.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.A.2.

items.question: If a non-compliant paddle is discovered before a match starts, there is no penalty to switch paddles.
items.explanationShort:
Before a match, the player just switches paddles.
During play, discovery means a match forfeit.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.A.1.

items.question: A player may hit the ball before it has fully crossed the plane of the net to their side.
items.explanationShort:
The ball has to fully cross the net plane first.
If you hit it early across the plane, it's a fault.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.F.1.

items.question: Graphics, insignias, pictures, and writing on apparel must be in good taste.
items.explanationShort:
Offensive content on clothing isn't permitted during play.
This includes language, symbols, or explicit images.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.B.1.

items.question: Touching the net with your paddle during a rally is allowed if accidental.
items.explanationShort:
Any contact with the net system during play is a fault.
This includes paddle, clothing, or anything carried.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.G.

items.question: In singles, the server serves from the right side when their score is zero or even.
items.explanationShort:
The server's score determines which side to serve from in singles.
An odd score means serving from the left side.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.A.1.

items.question: In singles, the server serves from the left side when their score is odd.
items.explanationShort:
In singles, the server's score determines the serving side.
An even score or zero means serving from the right side.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.A.1.

items.question: A player who refuses to change inappropriate apparel may forfeit their match.
items.explanationShort:
The Tournament Director may impose a match forfeit for non-compliance.
Refusing an apparel change is treated as a rules violation.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 18.B.4.

items.question: In singles, the score is called as three numbers.
items.explanationShort:
Singles uses two numbers: server's then receiver's.
Three numbers are used in standard doubles only.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.B.

items.question: In standard doubles, the score is called as three numbers.
items.explanationShort:
Server's score, then receiver's, then the server number.
Example: 'four–two–one' = server team 4, receivers 2.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 6.C.

items.question: In doubles, except when serving or receiving, players may stand anywhere on the court.
items.explanationShort:
No position restrictions exist after serve and return.
Only server and receiver have starting positions.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 5.B.4.

items.question: A returned ball that hits the net support system on the hitter's side is a fault.
items.explanationShort:
The net support system isn't the same as the net.
Only net contact itself keeps the ball in play.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 13.B.1.

items.question: In mini-singles, only half the court is in play during each rally.
items.explanationShort:
The in-play side depends on each player's score.
The centerline acts as the third boundary line.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 14.B.1.a.

items.question: A technical warning results in a point being deducted from the offender's score.
items.explanationShort:
A technical warning doesn't affect the score.
Only a technical foul deducts a point.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 22.C.1.

items.question: A technical foul results in one point being deducted from the offender's score.
items.explanationShort:
One point is removed from the offender's score.
If the score is zero, a point goes to the opponent.
2026 USA Pickleball Official Rulebook, Rule 22.E.1.
